title: Debugging - Running Flask in Development Mode with Debug Features Enabled

content.problemStatement:
As you start building your Flask applications, it's crucial to have a smooth development process. \
One of the key features Flask offers to assist developers is the ability to run the application in development mode with debugging enabled. \
This feature is especially helpful for beginners as it provides immediate feedback and makes troubleshooting easier.

When you run Flask in development mode with debug features enabled, you get two main benefits:

1. Auto-reloading: The server automatically restarts whenever you make changes to your code. \
This means you don't have to manually stop and restart the server each time you modify your application.

2. Interactive debugger: When an error occurs, Flask provides an interactive web-based debugger that shows you exactly where the error happened and allows you to inspect the code and variables at that point.

To enable these features, you need to set two configuration options when running your Flask application:

1. Set `debug=True`
   - By setting debug=True, you're telling Flask to run in development mode and enable the debugging features. 
   - This should only be used during development and never in a production environment, as it can expose sensitive information about your application.
2. Set `use_reloader=True` (this is typically enabled by default when debug is True)

You can set these options in your Python script or when running Flask from the command line. \
Here's how you can do it in your app.py file:

```python
from flask import Flask

app = Flask(__name__)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=True)
```
